title: Purely Pickled Argentinian White Yam Empanadas
description: An herbal and adventurous Argentinian dish featuring white yam, yuzu-cure, and ayurvedic-rajasic-friendly ingredients, resulting in a glassy-jelly texture and unique flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine diced white yam, yuzu juice, pickling spice, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the yam mixture onto the center of each empanada dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with olive oil and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the empanada and use a fork to crimp the edges.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and brush the tops with olive oil.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve warm.
